title: Session Polling & Event Handling
description: Polling for session invites, consuming session events, and listing active sessions via SessionHandler

activities.name: pollInvites
steps: Route receives request with optional body.limit
If yes: call sessionHandler.pollInvites({ limit: body.limit })
→ SessionHandler calls store.poll({ type: 'collaboration_invite', limit: limit || 10 })
If no sessionHandler: store.poll({ type: 'collaboration_invite', limit: body.limit || 10 }) directly

activities.name: pollSessionEvents
activities.trigger: Internal / direct call (no HTTP route defined)
steps: SessionHandler calls store.poll({ type: 'session_event', limit: limit || 20 })
Returns array of session event messages
conditions: No HTTP route registered — called programmatically

activities.name: listActiveSessions
steps: Route receives request with optional query.limit
→ SessionHandler calls store.list({ type: 'session_create', direction: 'outbound', limit: 50 })
→ Returns array of outbound session_create messages (= active sessions)
If no sessionHandler: store.list({ type: 'session_create', direction: 'outbound', limit: query.limit || 20 }) directly
conditions: sessionHandler present? → use SessionHandler path (limit fixed at 50)
sessionHandler absent? → use store directly, limit from query param (default 20)

branching_logic: All three polling/listing routes share the same guard pattern: if (sessionHandler) → delegate, else → fallback to store
pollInvites: limit defaults to 10 if not provided
pollSessionEvents: limit defaults to 20 if not provided
listActiveSessions: always filters direction='outbound' and type='session_create'
data_flow: pollInvites: inbound collaboration_invite messages → filtered by type → returned as message array
pollSessionEvents: session_event messages → filtered by type → returned as event array
listActiveSessions: outbound session_create messages → treated as session registry → returned as sessions array
store_operations: store.poll({ type: 'collaboration_invite', limit }) — used by pollInvites
store.poll({ type: 'session_event', limit }) — used by pollSessionEvents
store.list({ type: 'session_create', direction: 'outbound', limit }) — used by listActiveSessions
key_relationships: buildRoutes() injects sessionHandler via extensions object
SessionHandler is instantiated with { store, logger }
important_notes: pollSessionEvents has no HTTP route — it is not exposed via the REST API, only callable programmatically
All three methods are read-only polling operations (no side effects / no store.send calls)
The fallback path in routes.js bypasses SessionHandler and hits store directly, providing resilience when handler is unavailable
listActiveSessions derives 'active' sessions from outbound session_create messages (no explicit session state tracking)
